key_contribution.tr: Pulmoner kan dolaşımını William Harvey'den 300 yıl önce keşfetti — kanın sağ ventrikülden sol ventriküle akciğerler yoluyla geçtiğini kanıtlayarak 1.400 yıllık Galen doktrinini çürüttü
key_contribution.ar: اكتشف الدورة الدموية الرئوية قبل وليام هارفي بثلاثة قرون

scholarly_context.en: Ibn al-Nafis's description of pulmonary circulation is embedded in his Commentary on Ibn Sina's Canon. He explicitly rejected Galen's claim that blood passes through invisible pores in the interventricular septum, instead demonstrating that blood must travel from the right ventricle to the lungs, mix with air, then return to the left ventricle. This discovery was unknown to European medicine until Alpago's 1547 Latin translation.
scholarly_context.tr: İbnü'n-Nefîs'in pulmoner dolaşım tanımı, İbn Sînâ'nın Kânûn şerhine gömülüdür. Galen'in kanın interventriküler septumda görünmez gözeneklerden geçtiği iddiasını açıkça reddetti.
scholarly_context.ar: وصف ابن النفيس للدورة الدموية الرئوية مضمّن في شرحه لقانون ابن سينا. رفض صراحة ادعاء جالينوس بوجود مسام في الحاجز بين البطينين

historiographical_note.en: Meyerhof (1935) rediscovered Ibn al-Nafis's priority in pulmonary circulation. The question of whether Michael Servetus (1553) and Realdo Colombo (1559) had independent access to this knowledge or whether it was transmitted via Andrea Alpago's translations remains debated.
historiographical_note.tr: Meyerhof (1935), İbnü'n-Nefîs'in pulmoner dolaşımdaki önceliğini yeniden keşfetti.

modern_parallel.en: Imagine discovering how the heart actually works — and having the discovery buried in a footnote for 300 years until someone else gets the credit.
modern_parallel.tr: Kalbin gerçekte nasıl çalıştığını keşfetmeyi — ve keşfin 300 yıl bir dipnotta gömülü kalıp birinin krediyi almasını düşünün.

inherited_from.what: Ibn Sina's Canon of Medicine (to critique it)

transmitted_to.what: Christianismi Restitutio describes pulmonary circulation (possible transmission)
De Motu Cordis completed the circulation model

references.alam: {'name_ar': 'ابن النفيس، علي بن أبي الحزم', 'death_hijri': 687, 'page': 'VIII/47', 'entry_summary_ar': 'علاء الدين، طبيب شامي مصري. خدم في المستشفى المنصوري بالقاهرة. اكتشف الدورة الدموية الصغرى', 'entry_summary_en': 'Ala al-Din, Syrian-Egyptian physician. Served at Mansuri Hospital in Cairo. Discovered pulmonary (lesser) circulation.'}

full_name.en: Taqi al-Din Muhammad ibn Ma'ruf al-Shami
full_name.ar: تقي الدين محمد بن معروف الشامي

title.en: Sidrat al-Muntahā (The Lotus Tree of the Uttermost Boundary)

significance.en: Star catalogue with observations from the Istanbul Observatory — contemporary with Tycho Brahe
significance.tr: İstanbul Rasathanesi gözlemleriyle yıldız kataloğu — Tycho Brahe ile çağdaş

significance.en: Described a rudimentary steam turbine and six-cylinder pump — earliest known steam engine concepts
significance.tr: İlkel bir buhar türbini ve altı silindirli pompa tanımladı — bilinen en eski buhar motoru kavramları

key_contribution.en: Built the Istanbul Observatory (1577) — contemporary with Tycho Brahe's Uraniborg — and described a rudimentary steam turbine decades before European equivalents. The observatory's destruction by conservative opposition in 1580 is one of the great 'what ifs' of science history
key_contribution.tr: İstanbul Rasathanesi'ni (1577) inşa etti — Tycho Brahe'nin Uraniborg'u ile çağdaş — ve Avrupa eşdeğerlerinden on yıllar önce ilkel bir buhar türbini tanımladı. Rasathanenin 1580'de muhafazakâr muhalefetle yıkılması bilim tarihinin büyük 'ya olsaydı'larından biridir
key_contribution.ar: بنى مرصد إسطنبول (١٥٧٧) — معاصرًا لأورانيبورغ تيكو براهي

scholarly_context.en: Taqi al-Din's Istanbul Observatory was arguably the most advanced astronomical facility of its time, with instruments rivaling Tycho Brahe's (built the same year, 1577). His observations were potentially more accurate than Brahe's for some stars. The observatory's demolition by Şeyhülislam's fatwa in 1580 — barely three years after construction — represents one of the most consequential acts of anti-scientific policy in history.
scholarly_context.tr: Takiyüddîn'in İstanbul Rasathanesi, muhtemelen çağının en gelişmiş astronomik tesisiydi. Şeyhülislam'ın 1580'deki fetvası ile yıkılması — inşaattan sadece üç yıl sonra — bilim karşıtı politikanın tarihteki en önemli eylemlerinden birini temsil eder.
scholarly_context.ar: مرصد تقي الدين في إسطنبول كان يضاهي أجهزة تيكو براهي. هدمه بفتوى شيخ الإسلام عام ١٥٨٠ يمثل من أخطر أعمال السياسات المعادية للعلم

historiographical_note.en: Sayılı's The Observatory in Islam (1960) first documented the Istanbul Observatory's significance. İhsanoğlu's later work (2004) demonstrated that Taqi al-Din's instruments were comparable in sophistication to Tycho's and that his observations were of similar precision.
historiographical_note.tr: Sayılı'nın The Observatory in Islam (1960) eseri İstanbul Rasathanesi'nin önemini ilk kez belgeledi. İhsanoğlu (2004) Takiyüddîn'in aletlerinin Tycho'nunkilere benzer sofistikasyonda olduğunu gösterdi.

modern_parallel.en: Imagine building the Hubble Space Telescope — and then having it demolished three years later because a political faction decided it was inappropriate. That's what happened to Taqi al-Din's observatory.
modern_parallel.tr: Hubble Uzay Teleskopunu inşa etmeyi düşünün — ve sonra bir siyasi hizip uygunsuz olduğuna karar verdiği için üç yıl sonra yıktırılmasını.

transmitted_to.what: Independent but contemporary astronomical program — possible indirect awareness
Despite destruction, manuscripts survived and influenced later Ottoman science

references.alam: {'name_ar': 'تقي الدين المصري', 'death_hijri': 993, 'page': 'II/87', 'entry_summary_ar': 'تقي الدين محمد بن معروف. فلكي ومهندس. بنى مرصد إسطنبول. هُدم المرصد بفتوى شيخ الإسلام', 'entry_summary_en': "Taqi al-Din Muhammad ibn Ma'ruf. Astronomer and engineer. Built the Istanbul Observatory. The observatory was demolished by the Şeyhülislam's fatwa."}

full_name.en: Koca Mimar Sinan Agha
full_name.ar: قوجه معمار سنان آغا

significance.en: Dome surpassed Hagia Sophia's diameter (31.22m) — his self-declared masterpiece
significance.tr: Kubbenin çapı (31,22m) Ayasofya'yı aştı — kendi ilan ettiği şaheseri

significance.en: 14-building complex: mosque, madrasas, hospital, bath, kitchen, caravanserai — a self-contained urban ecosystem
significance.tr: 14 yapılı külliye: cami, medreseler, hastane, hamam, mutfak, kervansaray — kendi kendine yeten kentsel ekosistem

key_contribution.en: Designed 374 structures in 50 years as chief architect — including the Süleymaniye Complex (14 buildings) and the Selimiye Mosque whose dome surpassed Hagia Sophia. He is the greatest architect of the Islamic world
key_contribution.tr: 50 yılda baş mimar olarak 374 yapı tasarladı — Süleymaniye Külliyesi (14 yapı) ve kubbesi Ayasofya'yı aşan Selimiye Camii dahil. İslam dünyasının en büyük mimarıdır
key_contribution.ar: صمّم ٣٧٤ منشأة في ٥٠ عامًا رئيسًا للمعماريين — بما فيها مسجد السليمية الذي تجاوزت قبّته آيا صوفيا

scholarly_context.en: Sinan's career spans the entire golden age of Ottoman architecture. His 374 structures include 94 mosques, 52 prayer rooms, 57 madrasas, 35 palaces, 22 mausoleums, 17 public kitchens, 3 hospitals, and 7 aqueducts. The Selimiye's achievement was not just dome diameter (31.22m vs Hagia Sophia's 31.87m in one direction) but the unified interior space without secondary supports — something Hagia Sophia's buttressing system could not achieve.
scholarly_context.tr: Sinân'ın kariyeri Osmanlı mimarisinin tüm altın çağını kapsar. 374 yapısı 94 cami, 52 mescit, 57 medrese, 35 saray, 22 türbe, 17 imaret, 3 hastane ve 7 su kemeri içerir.
scholarly_context.ar: حياة سنان المهنية تمتد عبر العصر الذهبي للعمارة العثمانية. إنجاز السليمية لم يكن مجرد قطر قبة بل فضاء داخلي موحد بلا دعامات ثانوية

historiographical_note.en: Kuran's Sinan the Grand Old Master of Ottoman Architecture (1987) established the most comprehensive architectural analysis. Necipoğlu's The Age of Sinan (2005) contextualized his work within Ottoman court culture and politics. His autobiography (Tezkiretü'l-Bünyân) is one of the earliest architect's memoirs.
historiographical_note.tr: Kuran'ın eseri (1987) en kapsamlı mimari analizi oluşturdu. Necipoğlu (2005) eserini Osmanlı saray kültürü ve siyaseti içinde bağlamsallaştırdı.

modern_parallel.en: Imagine a single architect designing 374 buildings — including one whose dome rivals St. Peter's Basilica — over a 50-year career, while simultaneously engineering aqueducts and bridges. He's Frank Lloyd Wright, Zaha Hadid, and a civil engineer, combined.
modern_parallel.tr: Tek bir mimarın 50 yıllık bir kariyerde 374 bina tasarlamasını düşünün — kubbesi Aziz Petrus Bazilikası'yla yarışan biri dahil — aynı zamanda su kemerleri ve köprüler inşa ederken.

inherited_from.what: Hagia Sophia's structural principles (dome on pendentives)

transmitted_to.what: Sultan Ahmed Mosque (Blue Mosque) continues Sinan's tradition
National architectural identity draws on Sinan's forms

references.alam: {'name_ar': 'سنان، المعمار', 'death_hijri': 996, 'page': 'III/166', 'entry_summary_ar': 'قوجه معمار سنان. أعظم معماري في تاريخ الدولة العثمانية. بنى ٣٧٤ بناءً في خمسين عامًا', 'entry_summary_en': 'Koca Mimar Sinan. Greatest architect in Ottoman history. Built 374 structures in fifty years.'}

full_name.en: Muhiddin Piri ibn Hacı Mehmed
full_name.ar: محيي الدين بيري بن حاجي محمد

significance.en: World map showing Americas with extraordinary accuracy — drawn from 20+ sources including a Columbus map now lost
significance.tr: Olağanüstü doğrulukla Amerikaları gösteren dünya haritası — artık kayıp bir Kolomb haritası dahil 20+ kaynaktan çizildi

significance.en: Detailed navigational atlas of the Mediterranean with 219 maps and sailing instructions
significance.tr: 219 harita ve seyrüsefer talimatıyla Akdeniz'in detaylı navigasyon atlası

key_contribution.en: His 1513 world map is one of the oldest surviving maps showing the Americas — drawn from 20+ sources including a now-lost map used by Columbus. The Kitab-ı Bahriye is the most comprehensive Mediterranean navigational atlas of its era
key_contribution.tr: 1513 dünya haritası Amerika'ları gösteren en eski hayatta kalan haritalardan biridir — Kolomb'un kullandığı artık kayıp bir harita dahil 20+ kaynaktan çizildi
key_contribution.ar: خريطته عام ١٥١٣ من أقدم الخرائط الباقية التي تُظهر الأمريكتين

scholarly_context.en: The 1513 Piri Reis map fragment (surviving western third) is remarkable for several reasons: it shows the South American coast with accuracy impossible from European sources alone, it explicitly cites its 20+ sources (including 'a map by the Genoese infidel called Colombo'), and some scholars have argued it shows Antarctic coastlines — though this is contested. The Kitab-ı Bahriye (219 maps, 1521/1526 editions) remained the standard Mediterranean pilot guide for Ottoman navigators for two centuries.
scholarly_context.tr: 1513 Pîrî Reis harita parçası (hayatta kalan batı üçte biri), Güney Amerika kıyısını yalnızca Avrupa kaynaklarından mümkün olmayan doğrulukla göstermesi, 20+ kaynağını açıkça belirtmesi ile dikkat çekicidir.
scholarly_context.ar: خريطة بيري ريس ١٥١٣ تُظهر الساحل الأمريكي الجنوبي بدقة تفوق المصادر الأوروبية وحدها. كتاب البحرية (٢١٩ خريطة) بقي دليل الملاحة المعياري لقرنين

historiographical_note.en: The map was rediscovered in 1929 at Topkapı Palace. Kahle (1933) first published it. McIntosh's Piri Reis Map of 1513 (2000) provided the most thorough analysis, debunking the Hapgood/Antarctic theories while confirming the map's genuine cartographic significance.
historiographical_note.tr: Harita 1929'da Topkapı Sarayı'nda yeniden keşfedildi. McIntosh (2000) Hapgood/Antarktika teorilerini çürütürken haritanın gerçek kartografik önemini doğruladı.

modern_parallel.en: Imagine making the most accurate satellite image of a continent — 500 years before satellites — by combining intelligence from 20 different sources, including one the original owner lost.
modern_parallel.tr: Bir kıtanın en doğru uydu görüntüsünü yapmayı düşünün — uydulardan 500 yıl önce — orijinal sahibinin kaybettiği biri dahil 20 farklı kaynağı birleştirerek.

inherited_from.what: Idrisi's cartographic tradition, Islamic portolan charts
Columbus's now-lost map, Portuguese navigational charts

transmitted_to.what: Kitab-ı Bahriye used as standard pilot guide

references.alam: {'name_ar': 'بيري ريس', 'death_hijri': 960, 'page': 'II/55', 'entry_summary_ar': 'محيي الدين بيري بن حاجي محمد. أمير البحر العثماني ورسام خرائط. رسم خريطة العالم عام ٩١٩ هـ', 'entry_summary_en': 'Muhiddin Piri ibn Haji Mehmed. Ottoman admiral and cartographer. Drew the world map in 919 AH (1513 CE).'}

full_name.en: Ala al-Din Ali ibn Muhammad al-Qushji
full_name.ar: علاء الدين علي بن محمد القوشجي

active_places.role: Head of Ottoman scientific academy under Fatih

significance.en: Argued for Earth's rotation as physically possible — advanced arguments later echoed by Copernicus
significance.tr: Dünya'nın dönüşünü fiziksel olarak mümkün olarak savundu — daha sonra Kopernik tarafından yankılanan argümanlar

significance.en: Comprehensive mathematics textbook presented to Mehmed the Conqueror
significance.tr: Fatih Sultan Mehmed'e sunulan kapsamlı matematik ders kitabı

key_contribution.en: Bridge between Samarkand and Istanbul scientific traditions — directed Ulugh Beg's Observatory, then brought Timurid astronomical knowledge to the Ottoman court. Argued for Earth's rotation decades before Copernicus
key_contribution.tr: Semerkand ile İstanbul bilimsel gelenekleri arasında köprü — Uluğ Bey'in Rasathanesi'ni yönetti, sonra Timurlu astronomik bilgisini Osmanlı sarayına getirdi. Kopernik'ten on yıllar önce Dünya'nın dönüşünü savundu
key_contribution.ar: جسر بين تقاليد سمرقند وإسطنبول العلمية — أدار مرصد أولوغ بك

scholarly_context.en: Ali Qushji represents the critical knowledge transfer from the Timurid to Ottoman scientific worlds. Trained at the Samarkand Observatory, he inherited the Maragha-Samarkand astronomical tradition (Tusi couple, non-Ptolemaic models) and brought it to Istanbul. His argument that Earth's rotation cannot be disproved by physics (only by astronomy) is strikingly similar to Copernicus's reasoning — raising questions about possible transmission.
scholarly_context.tr: Ali Kuşçu, Timurlu'dan Osmanlı bilim dünyasına kritik bilgi transferini temsil eder. Semerkand Rasathanesi'nde yetişti, Meraga-Semerkand astronomi geleneğini miras alıp İstanbul'a getirdi.
scholarly_context.ar: علي قوشجي يمثل نقل المعرفة الحرج من العالم العلمي التيموري إلى العثماني. حجته بأن دوران الأرض لا يمكن دحضه بالفيزياء تشابه تفكير كوبرنيكوس

historiographical_note.en: Ragep (2001, 2007) explored the possible transmission chain from Ali Qushji to Copernicus via Italian intermediaries. Fazlıoğlu (2014) documented Qushji's institutional impact on Ottoman scientific education.
historiographical_note.tr: Ragep (2001, 2007), Ali Kuşçu'dan Kopernik'e İtalyan aracılar yoluyla olası aktarım zincirini araştırdı.

modern_parallel.en: Imagine a lead NASA scientist fleeing political turmoil, bringing classified space research to a rival power, and single-handedly establishing their space program — while also proving Earth rotates before anyone else believed it.
modern_parallel.tr: Siyasi kargaşadan kaçan baş NASA bilim insanının, gizli uzay araştırmalarını rakip bir güce getirip tek başına uzay programını kurmasını düşünün.

inherited_from.what: Nasir al-Din al-Tusi's non-Ptolemaic models (Tusi couple)
Ulugh Beg's star catalogue and observatory methods

transmitted_to.what: Tusi couple and non-Ptolemaic models may have reached Copernicus via Italian manuscripts

references.alam: {'name_ar': 'علي قوشجي', 'death_hijri': 879, 'page': 'IV/321', 'entry_summary_ar': 'علاء الدين، فلكي ورياضي. أدار مرصد أولوغ بك في سمرقند ثم انتقل إلى إسطنبول', 'entry_summary_en': "Ala al-Din, astronomer and mathematician. Directed Ulugh Beg's observatory in Samarkand then moved to Istanbul."}

full_name.en: Abu al-Hasan Ali ibn al-Husayn al-Masudi
full_name.ar: أبو الحسن علي بن الحسين المسعودي

active_places.role: Final years; wrote Meadows of Gold

significance.en: 30-volume world history from creation to 947 — the 'Arab Herodotus' combining geography, ethnography and history
significance.tr: Yaratılıştan 947'ye dünya tarihi — coğrafya, etnografya ve tarihi birleştiren 'Arapların Herodotos'u

key_contribution.en: The 'Arab Herodotus' — traveled from Baghdad to Persia, India, Sri Lanka, possibly China, Africa and back, producing the most vivid ethnographic survey of the 10th-century world
key_contribution.tr: 'Arapların Herodotos'u — Bağdat'tan İran, Hindistan, Sri Lanka, belki Çin ve Afrika'ya seyahat ederek 10. yüzyıl dünyasının en canlı etnografik araştırmasını üretti
key_contribution.ar: هيرودوت العرب — سافر من بغداد إلى فارس والهند وسريلانكا وأفريقيا

scholarly_context.en: Masudi broke the mould of Islamic historiography by combining geography, ethnography, natural history and comparative civilizational analysis. He examined seven pre-Islamic civilizations (Persian, Chaldean, Greek, Egyptian, Turkish, Chinese, Indian) with analytical detachment. Hugh Kennedy calls Meadows of Gold 'probably the best introduction to the Arabic historical tradition for the non-specialist.'
scholarly_context.tr: Mesûdî, coğrafya, etnografya, doğa tarihi ve karşılaştırmalı medeniyet analizini birleştirerek İslam tarihyazımının kalıplarını kırdı.
scholarly_context.ar: كسر المسعودي قالب التأريخ الإسلامي بالجمع بين الجغرافيا والإثنوغرافيا والتاريخ الطبيعي. فحص سبع حضارات قبل إسلامية بتجرد تحليلي

historiographical_note.en: Ibn Khaldun explicitly credited Masudi as a model historian in the Muqaddimah, stating he 'became an imam for historians.' De Meynard's 1861 French translation introduced Masudi to Western scholarship.
historiographical_note.tr: İbn Haldûn, Mukaddime'de Mesûdî'yi model tarihçi olarak alıntılayarak 'tarihçiler için imam oldu' dedi.

modern_parallel.en: Imagine combining a BBC world documentary with a Lonely Planet guide and a Smithsonian anthropology report — then writing it all by hand while traveling on foot and camelback for 30 years.
modern_parallel.tr: BBC dünya belgeselini, Lonely Planet rehberini ve Smithsonian antropoloji raporunu birleştirip 30 yıl boyunca deve sırtında seyahat ederek elle yazdığınızı düşünün.

transmitted_to.what: Muqaddimah credits Masudi as model historian

references.alam: {'name_ar': 'المسعودي، علي بن الحسين', 'death_hijri': 346, 'page': 'IV/276'}

full_name.en: Muhammad ibn Jarir al-Tabari
full_name.ar: محمد بن جرير الطبري

title.en: Jāmiʿ al-Bayān ʿan Taʾwīl Āy al-Qurʾān (Tafsir al-Tabari)
title.ar: جامع البيان عن تأويل آي القرآن

significance.en: The definitive Quran commentary — standard reference for 1,100+ years
significance.tr: Kesin Kur'an tefsiri — 1.100+ yıldır standart referans

title.en: Tārīkh al-Rusul wa-l-Mulūk (History of Prophets and Kings)

significance.en: World history from creation to 915 — 40-volume SUNY English translation
significance.tr: Yaratılıştan 915'e dünya tarihi — 40 ciltlik SUNY İngilizce çevirisi

key_contribution.en: Wrote the definitive Quran commentary AND a 40-volume world history — reportedly producing 40 pages per day for 40 years. His intellectual honesty set the standard for Islamic scholarship
key_contribution.tr: Kesin Kur'an tefsirini VE 40 ciltlik dünya tarihini yazdı — 40 yıl boyunca günde 40 sayfa ürettiği rivayet edilir
key_contribution.ar: كتب التفسير الأشمل للقرآن وتاريخًا عالميًا في ٤٠ مجلدًا

scholarly_context.en: Tabari's intellectual honesty was remarkable — he included contradictory accounts without editorial judgment, letting sources speak for themselves. His tafsir remains the standard reference for all subsequent Quran commentaries. Hugh Kennedy calls his history 'one of the great monuments of world history.'
scholarly_context.tr: Taberî'nin entelektüel dürüstlüğü dikkat çekiciydi — çelişkili rivayetleri editoryal yargı olmadan dahil etti.
scholarly_context.ar: أمانة الطبري الفكرية مميزة — أدرج روايات متناقضة دون حكم. تفسيره لا يزال المرجع المعياري. يصفه هيو كينيدي بأنه 'من الآثار العظيمة للتاريخ العالمي'

historiographical_note.en: SUNY's 40-volume English translation (completed after 20+ years) confirmed Tabari's indispensability. His citation of the 'Satanic Verses' incident became central to the Salman Rushdie controversy.
historiographical_note.tr: SUNY'nin 40 ciltlik İngilizce çevirisi Taberî'nin vazgeçilmezliğini doğruladı.

modern_parallel.en: Imagine writing both the Oxford Bible Commentary and the Encyclopedia Britannica's world history — by hand, alone, producing 40 pages a day for 40 years.
modern_parallel.tr: Hem Oxford Bible Commentary'yi hem Britannica dünya tarihini — elle, tek başınıza, 40 yıl boyunca günde 40 sayfa yazarak üretmeyi düşünün.

transmitted_to.what: De Goeje's critical edition of the History

references.alam: {'name_ar': 'الطبري، محمد بن جرير', 'death_hijri': 310, 'page': 'VI/69'}

full_name.en: Muhammad ibn Muhammad ibn al-Hasan al-Tusi
full_name.tr: Muhammed b. Muhammed b. el-Hasen et-Tûsî
full_name.ar: محمد بن محمد بن الحسن الطوسي

active_places.role: Founded and directed the Maragha Observatory

title.en: Al-Tadhkira fī ʿIlm al-Hayʾa (Memoir on Astronomy)

significance.en: Non-Ptolemaic planetary models; the 'Tusi couple' mathematical device appears almost identically in Copernicus
significance.tr: Ptolemaios-dışı gezegen modelleri; 'Tûsî çifti' matematiksel aracı Kopernik'te neredeyse birebir görülür

significance.en: Most widely read ethical treatise in the Islamic world
significance.tr: İslam dünyasının en çok okunan ahlak risalesi

key_contribution.en: Invented the 'Tusi couple' — a mathematical device that appears almost identically in Copernicus's De Revolutionibus 300 years later. Founded the Maragha Observatory, the most advanced astronomical facility of the medieval world. Described the Milky Way as composed of countless tiny stars — 300 years before Galileo proved it with a telescope
key_contribution.tr: 'Tûsî çifti'ni icat etti — 300 yıl sonra Kopernik'in De Revolutionibus'unda neredeyse birebir görülen matematiksel araç. Ortaçağ dünyasının en gelişmiş astronomik tesisi Meraga Rasathanesi'ni kurdu
key_contribution.ar: اخترع 'زوج الطوسي' الذي يظهر في عمل كوبرنيكوس بعد ٣٠٠ عام. أسّس مرصد مراغة

scholarly_context.en: Tusi is considered the greatest astronomer between Ptolemy and Copernicus. His Tusi couple generated linear motion from circular orbits — solving a mathematical problem Ptolemy could not. The question of whether Copernicus had access to Tusi's work (via Byzantine Greek intermediaries) is one of the most debated in history of science. He also described the Milky Way as composed of tightly-clustered small stars — Galileo proved this 300 years later.
scholarly_context.tr: Tûsî, Batlamyus ile Kopernik arasındaki en büyük astronom kabul edilir. Tûsî çifti dairesel yörüngelerden doğrusal hareket üretti.
scholarly_context.ar: الطوسي أعظم فلكي بين بطليموس وكوبرنيكوس. زوجه الطوسي يولّد حركة خطية من مدارات دائرية. وصف درب التبانة بأنها نجوم صغيرة متراصة — أثبتها غاليليو بعد ٣٠٠ عام

historiographical_note.en: Ragep (1993) provided the definitive study of Tusi's astronomical works. The Tusi-to-Copernicus transmission question remains the most discussed problem in Islamic science historiography (Saliba 2007, Di Bono 1995).
historiographical_note.tr: Ragep (1993) Tûsî'nin astronomik eserlerinin kesin çalışmasını sağladı.

modern_parallel.en: Imagine a genius captured by a terrorist organization, then switching sides to the conquering army, and using the conqueror's money to build the most advanced research laboratory in the world — all while solving a mathematical problem that wouldn't be matched for 300 years.
modern_parallel.tr: Bir terörist örgüt tarafından tutulan, sonra fatih ordusunun tarafına geçen ve fatihin parasıyla dünyanın en gelişmiş araştırma laboratuvarını kuran bir dahi düşünün.

inherited_from.what: Khwarizmi's algebra, Battani's observations, Ibn al-Haytham's critique of Ptolemy

transmitted_to.what: Tusi couple geometry appears in De Revolutionibus

references.alam: {'name_ar': 'الطوسي، محمد بن محمد', 'death_hijri': 672, 'page': 'VII/23'}

full_name.en: Abu Nuwas al-Hasan ibn Hani al-Hakami
full_name.tr: Ebû Nüvâs el-Hasen b. Hânî el-Hakemî
full_name.ar: أبو نواس الحسن بن هانئ الحكمي

significance.en: Revolutionized Arabic poetry — replaced desert nostalgia with urban hedonism, still banned in parts of the Arab world
significance.tr: Arap şiirini devrimleştirdi — çöl nostaljisini kentsel hedonizmle değiştirdi, Arap dünyasının bazı bölgelerinde hâlâ yasaklı

key_contribution.en: Greatest Arabic-language poet — scandalised courts with wine and love poems, appeared as character in 1001 Nights, still banned in parts of the Arab world 1,200 years later
key_contribution.tr: En büyük Arapça şair — şarap ve aşk şiirleriyle sarayları skandalize etti, 1001 Gece'de karakter olarak göründü, 1.200 yıl sonra hâlâ yasaklı
key_contribution.ar: أعظم شاعر بالعربية — أثار الجدل بقصائد الخمر والغزل

scholarly_context.en: Abu Nuwas represents the radical cultural freedom of Abbasid Baghdad. His khamriyyat (wine poetry) deliberately broke with pre-Islamic qasida conventions, replacing desert nostalgia with urban sophistication. The source notes he spent most of his life 'in pursuit not of progress, but of pleasure.' He remains controversial: still banned in some countries, yet considered by many scholars the finest Arabic poet ever.
scholarly_context.tr: Ebû Nüvâs, Abbasi Bağdat'ının radikal kültürel özgürlüğünü temsil eder.
scholarly_context.ar: يمثل أبو نواس الحرية الثقافية الراديكالية لبغداد العباسية. خمرياته كسرت عمدًا تقاليد القصيدة الجاهلية

historiographical_note.en: Wagner's Abu Nuwas (1965) established the critical biography. His appearance in 1001 Nights cemented popular fame but obscured the literary sophistication that makes him a major world poet.
historiographical_note.tr: Wagner'in Abu Nuwas (1965) çalışması kritik biyografiyi oluşturdu.

modern_parallel.en: Imagine a court poet who is simultaneously the Bob Dylan, Oscar Wilde, and Charles Bukowski of his civilization — celebrated, banned, and immortalized in folklore, all at once.
modern_parallel.tr: Aynı anda medeniyetinin Bob Dylan'ı, Oscar Wilde'ı ve Charles Bukowski'si olan bir saray şairi düşünün.

inherited_from.what: Sasanian court poetry and wine culture

references.alam: {'name_ar': 'أبو نواس، الحسن بن هانئ', 'death_hijri': 199, 'page': 'II/79'}

full_name.en: Abu Ali al-Hasan ibn Ali al-Tusi
full_name.tr: Ebû Alî el-Hasen b. Alî et-Tûsî
full_name.ar: أبو علي الحسن بن علي الطوسي

active_places.role: Vizier to Alp Arslan and Malik Shah for 30 years

significance.en: 50-chapter treatise on statecraft in the Persian 'Mirrors for Princes' tradition — blueprint for Seljuk and later Islamic governance
significance.tr: Fars 'Hükümdarlara Nasihat' geleneğinde 50 bölümlük devlet yönetimi risalesi

key_contribution.en: Created the Nizamiyya madrasa network — the first systematically organized chain of higher education institutions in the Islamic world, model for the European university. Served as vizier for 30 years across two sultans, transforming the Seljuk state
key_contribution.tr: Nizâmiye medrese ağını kurdu — İslam dünyasının ilk sistematik yüksek öğretim kurumları zinciri, Avrupa üniversitesinin modeli. 30 yıl iki sultana vezirlik yaparak Selçuklu devletini dönüştürdü
key_contribution.ar: أسّس شبكة المدارس النظامية — أول سلسلة منظمة للتعليم العالي في العالم الإسلامي

scholarly_context.en: Many historians call Nizam al-Mulk 'the most important statesman in Islamic history.' His Nizamiyya network was a deliberate Sunni intellectual counter-offensive against Fatimid Ismaili proselytism via al-Azhar. The Baghdad Nizamiyya housed 3,000 students at its peak under Ghazali. His iqta land-grant system influenced the Ottoman timar. Assassinated by a Hasan-i Sabbah fidai on Oct 14, 1092.
scholarly_context.tr: Birçok tarihçi Nizâmülmülk'ü 'İslam tarihinin en önemli devlet adamı' olarak adlandırır.
scholarly_context.ar: يسميه كثير من المؤرخين 'أهم رجل دولة في التاريخ الإسلامي.' شبكة النظامية كانت هجومًا فكريًا سنيًا مضادًا للدعاية الإسماعيلية الفاطمية

historiographical_note.en: Lambton's 1984 chapter in Cambridge History of Iran and Darke's 1960 translation of the Siyasatnama remain standard references. The legendary (but apocryphal) schoolmate story linking Nizam, Khayyam, and Hasan-i Sabbah has been debunked — Nizam was 32 years older than Sabbah.
historiographical_note.tr: Lambton'un 1984 bölümü ve Darke'ın 1960 Siyâsetnâme çevirisi standart referanslardır.

modern_parallel.en: Imagine a single person building Harvard, Yale, Princeton, and Oxford — all at once, while simultaneously running the Defense Department, reforming the tax code, and writing the definitive book on governance. Then getting assassinated by a dagger-wielding fanatic.
modern_parallel.tr: Tek bir kişinin Harvard, Yale, Princeton ve Oxford'u aynı anda kurmasını düşünün — savunma bakanlığını yönetirken, vergi sistemini reforme ederken ve yönetim üzerine kesin kitabı yazarken.

inherited_from.what: Sasanian administrative tradition, 'Mirrors for Princes' genre

transmitted_to.what: Iqta system influenced Ottoman land tenure

references.alam: {'name_ar': 'نظام الملك، الحسن بن علي', 'death_hijri': 485, 'page': 'VIII/45'}

full_name.en: Abu al-Fath Abd al-Rahman al-Khazini
full_name.ar: أبو الفتح عبد الرحمن الخازني

active_places.role: Court astronomer; wrote Book of the Balance of Wisdom

title.en: Kitāb Mīzān al-Ḥikma (Book of the Balance of Wisdom)

significance.en: 8-volume encyclopedia of mechanics and hydrostatics — measured specific gravities of 50 substances with precision unsurpassed until the 20th century
significance.tr: 8 ciltlik mekanik ve hidrostatik ansiklopedisi — 50 maddenin özgül ağırlığını 20. yüzyıla kadar aşılmayan hassasiyetle ölçtü

key_contribution.en: A former Byzantine slave who built a hydrostatic balance precise to the microgram, measured specific gravities of 50 substances, and proposed that gravity varies with distance from Earth's center — 500 years before Newton
key_contribution.tr: Mikrogram hassasiyetinde hidrostatik terazi inşa eden, 50 maddenin özgül ağırlığını ölçen ve yerçekiminin Dünya merkezine uzaklıkla değiştiğini öneren eski bir Bizans kölesi — Newton'dan 500 yıl önce
key_contribution.ar: عبد بيزنطي سابق بنى ميزانًا هيدروستاتيكيًا بدقة الميكروغرام

scholarly_context.en: Al-Khazini was a Byzantine Greek captured after Manzikert (1071), educated by his master, and studied under Khayyam himself. His Book of the Balance of Wisdom is described as 'the most comprehensive work on weighing in the Middle Ages, from any cultural area.' He proposed gravity varies with distance from Earth's center — not systematically explored again until Newton. He lived in extreme asceticism, refusing 1,000 dinars and living on 3 dinars per year.
scholarly_context.tr: Hâzinî, Malazgirt (1071) sonrası esir alınan bir Bizans Rum'uydu. Kitâbü Mîzâni'l-Hikme 'ortaçağın her kültür alanından en kapsamlı tartı eseri' olarak tanımlanır.
scholarly_context.ar: الخازني بيزنطي أُسر بعد ملاذكرد. كتاب ميزان الحكمة 'أشمل عمل عن الموازين في العصور الوسطى.' اقترح تغيّر الجاذبية بالمسافة — قبل نيوتن بخمسة قرون

historiographical_note.en: Khanikoff's 1859 partial translation first introduced Khazini to Western science history. Ibel (1908) and Hall (1981) confirmed the unprecedented precision of his measurements.
historiographical_note.tr: Khanikoff'un 1859 kısmi çevirisi Hâzinî'yi Batı bilim tarihine tanıttı.

modern_parallel.en: A captured slave who became one of the greatest experimental physicists in history — building instruments more precise than anything in Europe for 800 years, while living on $3 a year and sharing his home only with a cat.
modern_parallel.tr: Tarihin en büyük deneysel fizikçilerinden biri olan esir bir köle — 800 yıl boyunca Avrupa'daki her şeyden daha hassas aletler inşa ederken yılda 3 dinarla yaşayan ve evini sadece bir kedisiyle paylaşan.

inherited_from.what: Omar Khayyam (personal teacher), Biruni's density measurements

references.alam: {'name_ar': 'الخازني، عبد الرحمن', 'death_hijri': 550, 'page': 'IV/24'}

full_name.en: Sharaf al-Din al-Muzaffar ibn Muhammad al-Tusi
full_name.ar: شرف الدين المظفر بن محمد الطوسي

significance.en: Extended Khayyam's cubic equation solutions — first implicit use of the derivative, 'beginning of algebraic geometry' (Rashed)
significance.tr: Hayyâm'ın kübik denklem çözümlerini genişletti — türevin ilk örtük kullanımı, 'cebirsel geometrinin başlangıcı' (Rashed)

key_contribution.en: Extended Khayyam's cubic equations by finding maxima of cubic functions — an implicit use of the derivative centuries before calculus. Roshdi Rashed called this 'the beginning of algebraic geometry'
key_contribution.tr: Kübik fonksiyonların maksimumlarını bularak Hayyâm'ın kübik denklemlerini genişletti — analizden yüzyıllar önce türevin örtük kullanımı
key_contribution.ar: وسّع معادلات الخيام التكعيبية — أول استخدام ضمني للمشتقة

scholarly_context.en: Sharaf al-Din's treatise classified all 25 types of cubic equations and investigated existence conditions for positive solutions. He applied the Ruffini-Horner method to approximate roots — the first known application to general polynomials. His student Kamal al-Din ibn Yunus taught Nasir al-Din al-Tusi, creating a direct lineage from Seljuk to Maragha Observatory.
scholarly_context.tr: Şerefeddin'in risalesi tüm 25 tip kübik denklemi sınıflandırdı.
scholarly_context.ar: صنّف شرف الدين جميع أنواع المعادلات المكعبة وبحث شروط وجود الحلول الموجبة. طبّق طريقة رفيني-هورنر على كثيرات الحدود العامة لأول مرة

historiographical_note.en: Rashed's 1986 critical edition and analysis revealed the revolutionary character of Sharaf al-Din's mathematics, previously unknown to Western scholarship.
historiographical_note.tr: Rashed'in 1986 kritik edisyonu Şerefeddin'in matematiğinin devrimci karakterini ortaya çıkardı.

modern_parallel.en: He invented calculus concepts 500 years before Newton and Leibniz — except nobody noticed until 1986.
modern_parallel.tr: Newton ve Leibniz'den 500 yıl önce analiz kavramlarını icat etti — ama 1986'ya kadar kimse fark etmedi.

transmitted_to.who: Kamal al-Din ibn Yunus → Nasir al-Din al-Tusi

references.alam: {'name_ar': 'الطوسي، شرف الدين', 'death_hijri': 610, 'page': 'IV/120'}

full_name.en: Abu Marwan Abd al-Malik ibn Zuhr
full_name.ar: أبو مروان عبد الملك بن زهر

active_places.role: Court physician to Almoravids and Almohads

significance.en: Practical clinical medicine companion to Ibn Rushd's theoretical Kulliyat — together they formed the complete medical reference
significance.tr: İbn Rüşd'ün teorik Külliyât'ına pratik klinik tıp tamamlayıcısı

key_contribution.en: First to describe pericardial abscesses, first to test medicines on animals before humans, pioneered tracheotomy and used catgut for internal sutures. His Taysir complemented Ibn Rushd's theoretical Kulliyat
key_contribution.tr: Perikard apselerini ilk tanımlayan, ilaçları insanlardan önce hayvanlarda test eden, trakeotomiye öncülük eden ve iç dikişlerde kedigüt kullanan kişi
key_contribution.ar: أول من وصف خراجات التامور وأول من اختبر الأدوية على الحيوانات

scholarly_context.en: Ibn Zuhr pioneered experimental pharmacology by systematically testing medicines on animals before administering them to humans — a practice not standardized in Europe until the 19th century. His Taysir was deliberately written as the practical companion to Ibn Rushd's theoretical Kulliyat, forming a complete medical system. He described laryngeal treatment and demonstrated that surgical incisions in the larynx could heal.
scholarly_context.tr: İbn Zühr, ilaçları insanlara vermeden önce sistematik olarak hayvanlarda test ederek deneysel farmakolojiye öncülük etti.
scholarly_context.ar: ريادة ابن زهر في الصيدلة التجريبية باختبار الأدوية على الحيوانات قبل البشر — لم تُقنن في أوروبا حتى القرن التاسع عشر. التيسير مع كليات ابن رشد شكّلا نظامًا طبيًا كاملًا

historiographical_note.en: Colin's 1911 study established Ibn Zuhr's priority in experimental pharmacology. The Taysir-Kulliyat partnership with Ibn Rushd is unique in medieval medical literature.
historiographical_note.tr: Colin'in 1911 çalışması İbn Zühr'ün deneysel farmakolojideki önceliğini belirledi.

modern_parallel.en: The first person to insist on animal testing before human drug trials — and also the surgeon who proved you could cut open a throat and it would heal. All in 12th-century Spain.
modern_parallel.tr: İnsan ilaç denemelerinden önce hayvan testinde ısrar eden ilk kişi — ve boğazı kesip iyileşeceğini kanıtlayan cerrah.

inherited_from.what: Razi's clinical methodology, family medical tradition (5 generations)

references.alam: {'name_ar': 'ابن زهر، عبد الملك', 'death_hijri': 557, 'page': 'III/300'}

full_name.en: Diya al-Din Abu Muhammad Abdullah ibn Ahmad ibn al-Baytar
full_name.tr: Ziyâeddin Ebû Muhammed Abdullah b. Ahmed İbnü'l-Beytâr
full_name.ar: ضياء الدين أبو محمد عبد الله بن أحمد ابن البيطار

significance.en: Greatest medieval pharmacological encyclopedia — 1,400 plants, 300 new to science
significance.tr: Ortaçağın en büyük farmakoloji ansiklopedisi — 1.400 bitki, bilime 300 yenisi

key_contribution.en: Greatest botanist and pharmacologist of the medieval world — catalogued 1,400 medicinal plants (300 entirely new to science), the first systematic parasitologist
key_contribution.tr: Ortaçağ dünyasının en büyük botanikçi ve farmakoloğu — 1.400 tıbbi bitkiyi katalogladı (bilime 300 tamamen yeni), ilk sistematik parazitolog
key_contribution.ar: أعظم عالم نبات وصيدلة في العالم الوسيط — فهرس ١,٤٠٠ نبتة طبية

scholarly_context.en: Ibn al-Baytar traveled from Málaga through North Africa to Egypt and the Levant, collecting plant specimens and documenting their medicinal properties. His Jami catalogued 1,400 plants with their uses in Arabic, Berber, Greek, Latin, Persian and Romance languages — 300 were entirely new to pharmacological literature.
scholarly_context.tr: İbnü'l-Beytâr, Mâleka'dan Kuzey Afrika üzerinden Mısır ve Levant'a seyahat ederek bitki örnekleri topladı.
scholarly_context.ar: سافر ابن البيطار من مالقة عبر شمال أفريقيا إلى مصر والشام جامعًا العينات النباتية. فهرس ١,٤٠٠ نبتة بستّ لغات — ٣٠٠ منها جديدة كليًا على الأدب الصيدلاني

historiographical_note.en: Leclerc's 1877-83 French translation established Ibn al-Baytar as the greatest medieval pharmacologist. Meyerhof (1933) documented the 300 original plant descriptions.
historiographical_note.tr: Leclerc'in 1877-83 Fransızca çevirisi İbnü'l-Beytâr'ı ortaçağın en büyük farmakoloğu olarak belirledi.

modern_parallel.en: Imagine walking from Spain to Egypt collecting every plant along the way, then writing the world's most comprehensive drug encyclopedia — in six languages.
modern_parallel.tr: İspanya'dan Mısır'a yürüyerek yol boyunca her bitkiyi toplayıp sonra dünyanın en kapsamlı ilaç ansiklopedisini — altı dilde — yazmayı düşünün.

transmitted_to.what: Manuscript tradition in Ottoman medical schools

references.alam: {'name_ar': 'ابن البيطار، عبد الله بن أحمد', 'death_hijri': 646, 'page': 'III/259'}

full_name.en: Taqi al-Din Ahmad ibn Ali al-Maqrizi
full_name.ar: تقي الدين أحمد بن علي المقريزي

active_places.role: Historian of Cairo and Mamluk state

significance.en: Most detailed topographical and historical description of medieval Cairo — invaluable for understanding Mamluk urban civilization
significance.tr: Ortaçağ Kahire'sinin en ayrıntılı topografik ve tarihsel tanımı

key_contribution.en: Produced the most detailed topographical history of Cairo ever written — documenting every mosque, madrasa, hospital, street and monument, making medieval Cairo the best-documented city in the pre-modern world
key_contribution.tr: Kahire'nin yazılmış en ayrıntılı topografik tarihini üretti — ortaçağ Kahire'sini modern öncesi dünyanın en iyi belgelenmiş şehri yaparak
key_contribution.ar: أنتج أكثر تاريخ طبوغرافي تفصيلي للقاهرة — جعل القاهرة الوسيطة أفضل مدينة موثّقة

scholarly_context.en: Maqrizi's Khitat documented every building, street, and institution in Cairo, creating what source material calls 'the best-documented medieval city.' His economic analysis of Mamluk fiscal policy anticipated modern economic history methodology.
scholarly_context.tr: Makrîzî'nin Hıtat'ı Kahire'deki her yapıyı, sokağı ve kurumu belgeledi.
scholarly_context.ar: وثّقت خطط المقريزي كل مبنى وشارع ومؤسسة في القاهرة. تحليله الاقتصادي للسياسة المالية المملوكية سبق منهجية التاريخ الاقتصادي الحديث

historiographical_note.en: Wiet's (1911-27) partial translation established Maqrizi's indispensability for Mamluk studies. Rabbat (2010) demonstrated his urban history methodology was centuries ahead of European equivalents.
historiographical_note.tr: Wiet'in (1911-27) kısmi çevirisi Makrîzî'nin Memlük çalışmaları için vazgeçilmezliğini belirledi.

modern_parallel.en: Imagine Google Street View — but done by hand, in the 15th century, documenting every building in the world's largest city.
modern_parallel.tr: Google Street View'ı düşünün — ama elle, 15. yüzyılda, dünyanın en büyük şehrindeki her yapıyı belgeleyerek.

transmitted_to.what: French scholars used Khitat as baseline

references.alam: {'name_ar': 'المقريزي، أحمد بن علي', 'death_hijri': 845, 'page': 'I/172'}

full_name.en: Jalal al-Din Abu al-Fadl Abd al-Rahman al-Suyuti
full_name.ar: جلال الدين أبو الفضل عبد الرحمن السيوطي

active_places.role: Most prolific Islamic author in history

significance.en: Definitive encyclopedia of Quranic sciences — still the standard reference
significance.tr: Kur'ân ilimlerinin kesin ansiklopedisi — hâlâ standart referans

significance.en: Complete history of the Islamic caliphate from Abu Bakr to the Mamluks
significance.tr: Ebû Bekir'den Memlüklere İslam halifeliğinin tam tarihi

key_contribution.en: Islam's most prolific author — wrote 500+ works covering Quranic sciences, hadith, linguistics, history, and natural sciences. His al-Itqan remains the definitive encyclopedia of Quranic studies
key_contribution.tr: İslam'ın en verimli yazarı — Kur'ân ilimleri, hadis, dilbilim, tarih ve doğa bilimlerini kapsayan 500+ eser yazdı
key_contribution.ar: أغزر مؤلفي الإسلام — كتب أكثر من ٥٠٠ عمل

scholarly_context.en: Suyuti is considered the last great polymath of classical Islam. His output of 500+ works is unmatched. Source material describes him as having written on 'sociology, philosophy of history, and economics' in addition to religious sciences. His Tafsir al-Jalalayn (co-authored) remains one of the most widely used Quran commentaries.
scholarly_context.tr: Süyûtî, klasik İslam'ın son büyük polimâtı kabul edilir. 500+ eserlik üretimi emsalsizdir.
scholarly_context.ar: السيوطي آخر عالم موسوعي عظيم في الإسلام الكلاسيكي. إنتاجه من ٥٠٠+ عمل لا مثيل له. والإتقان لا يزال المرجع الحاسم لعلوم القرآن

historiographical_note.en: Sartain's 1975 study documented the full bibliography. GAS identifies him as the mujaddid (renewer) of the 9th Islamic century. His role as 'last encyclopedist' marks the transition from classical to early modern Islamic scholarship.
historiographical_note.tr: Sartain'ın 1975 çalışması tam bibliyografyayı belgeledi.

modern_parallel.en: Imagine writing 500 books covering everything from theology to linguistics to natural history — by hand, in one lifetime. He was Wikipedia before Wikipedia.
modern_parallel.tr: Teolojiden dilbilime, doğa tarihine kadar her şeyi kapsayan 500 kitap yazmayı düşünün — elle, tek bir ömürde.

transmitted_to.what: Widely copied and studied across the Islamic world
Itqan remains the standard Quranic sciences reference

references.alam: {'name_ar': 'السيوطي، عبد الرحمن', 'death_hijri': 911, 'page': 'III/301'}

full_name.en: Evliya ibn Derviş Mehmed Zılli
full_name.ar: أوليا بن درويش محمد ظلّي

significance.en: 10-volume travel chronicle covering the entire Ottoman world — richest source for 17th-century Ottoman social history
significance.tr: Tüm Osmanlı dünyasını kapsayan 10 ciltlik seyahat kroniği — 17. yüzyıl Osmanlı sosyal tarihinin en zengin kaynağı

key_contribution.en: Wrote the 10-volume Seyahatnâme — traveling for 40+ years across the Ottoman Empire and beyond, documenting cities, customs, foods, music, architecture and daily life. The richest source for Ottoman social history
key_contribution.tr: 10 ciltlik Seyâhatnâme'yi yazdı — 40+ yıl boyunca Osmanlı İmparatorluğu'nda seyahat ederek şehirleri, adetleri, yemekleri belgeledi
key_contribution.ar: كتب سياحتنامه في ١٠ مجلدات — سافر أكثر من ٤٠ عامًا

scholarly_context.en: Source material describes the Seyahatnâme as '10-volume' covering '40+ years of travel' and calls it 'the richest source for Ottoman social history.' Evliya's descriptions of foods, music, crafts, architecture and daily life across 3 continents make his work uniquely valuable for social historians.
scholarly_context.tr: Kaynak materyali Seyâhatnâme'yi '10 ciltlik' ve '40+ yıllık seyahati' kapsayan olarak tanımlar.
scholarly_context.ar: سياحتنامه ١٠ مجلدات تغطي ٤٠+ عامًا من السفر. وصفه للأطعمة والموسيقى والحرف والعمارة والحياة اليومية عبر ثلاث قارات يجعله فريدًا للمؤرخين الاجتماعيين

historiographical_note.en: The complete critical edition is still ongoing (Yapı Kredi Yayınları). Dankoff's partial English translations (2004-) introduced Evliya to Western scholarship. His reliability varies — he embellishes, but core observations are confirmed archaeologically.
historiographical_note.tr: Tam kritik edisyon hâlâ devam ediyor (Yapı Kredi). Dankoff'un kısmi İngilizce çevirileri Evliya'yı Batı akademisine tanıttı.

modern_parallel.en: A 17th-century travel blogger with 40 years of content, covering everything from architecture reviews to restaurant guides to ethnographic surveys — all in 10 handwritten volumes.
modern_parallel.tr: 40 yıllık içerikle 17. yüzyıl seyahat blogcusu — mimari eleştirilerden restoran rehberlerine, etnografik araştırmalara kadar her şeyi kapsayan 10 el yazması cilt.

inherited_from.what: Ibn Battuta and Ibn Jubayr rihla tradition

transmitted_to.what: Primary source for Ottoman social/cultural history
Seyahatnâme included in Memory of the World Register

references.alam: {'name_ar': 'أوليا جلبي', 'death_hijri': 1093, 'page': 'II/358'}

full_name.en: Abu Nasr Muhammad ibn Muhammad al-Farabi
full_name.tr: Ebû Nasr Muhammed b. Muhammed el-Fârâbî
full_name.ar: أبو نصر محمد بن محمد الفارابي

active_places.role: Studied under Yuhanna ibn Haylan; philosophical career

significance.en: Most important medieval treatise on music theory — influenced both Islamic and European musical traditions
significance.tr: Ortaçağın en önemli müzik teorisi risalesi

title.en: Ārāʾ Ahl al-Madīna al-Fāḍila (The Virtuous City)

significance.en: Islamic political philosophy synthesizing Plato's Republic with Islamic governance
significance.tr: Platon'un Devlet'ini İslami yönetimle sentezleyen siyasi felsefe

key_contribution.en: The 'Second Teacher' (after Aristotle) — synthesized Plato and Aristotle into an Islamic philosophical framework that Ibn Sina and Ibn Rushd built upon. Also wrote the most important medieval treatise on music theory
key_contribution.tr: 'İkinci Muallim' (Aristoteles'ten sonra) — Platon ve Aristoteles'i İbn Sînâ ve İbn Rüşd'ün üzerine inşa ettiği İslami felsefi çerçevede sentezledi. Ayrıca ortaçağın en önemli müzik teorisi risalesini yazdı
key_contribution.ar: المعلم الثاني (بعد أرسطو) — جمع بين أفلاطون وأرسطو في إطار فلسفي إسلامي بنى عليه ابن سينا وابن رشد. وكتب أهم رسالة في نظرية الموسيقى

scholarly_context.en: Farabi created the philosophical vocabulary and methodology that defined Islamic philosophy for 500 years. His emanationist cosmology harmonized Aristotelian logic with Islamic monotheism. Ibn Sina called him the greatest philosopher after Aristotle — hence 'Second Teacher.'
scholarly_context.tr: Fârâbî, 500 yıl boyunca İslam felsefesini belirleyen felsefi terminoloji ve metodolojiyi yarattı.
scholarly_context.ar: أنشأ الفارابي المصطلحات والمنهجية الفلسفية التي حددت الفلسفة الإسلامية لخمسة قرون. وفّق بين المنطق الأرسطي والتوحيد الإسلامي من خلال نظرية الفيض

historiographical_note.en: Walzer's 1985 critical edition of al-Madina al-Fadila reestablished Farabi's originality. Mahdi (2001) demonstrated his political philosophy was more than Platonic imitation.
historiographical_note.tr: Walzer'in 1985 kritik edisyonu Fârâbî'nin özgünlüğünü yeniden kurdu.

modern_parallel.en: Imagine being so good at explaining Aristotle that everyone calls you 'Second Aristotle' — and then also writing the definitive textbook on music theory as a side project.
modern_parallel.tr: Aristoteles'i açıklamakta o kadar iyi olmayı düşünün ki herkes size 'İkinci Aristoteles' desin — ve yan proje olarak müzik teorisi ders kitabını da yazın.

transmitted_to.what: Political philosophy and Aristotelian commentary tradition
Guide for the Perplexed draws on Farabi's framework

references.alam: {'name_ar': 'الفارابي، محمد بن محمد', 'death_hijri': 339, 'page': 'VII/202'}

full_name.en: Abu al-Hasan Thabit ibn Qurra al-Harrani
full_name.ar: أبو الحسن ثابت بن قرة الحراني

active_places.role: House of Wisdom translator and mathematician

title.en: Arabic translations of Archimedes, Apollonius, and Euclid
title.tr: Arşimet, Apollonios ve Öklid'in Arapça çevirileri
title.ar: ترجمات أرخميدس وأبولونيوس وإقليدس إلى العربية

significance.en: Preserved Greek mathematical masterworks — many survive only in his Arabic versions
significance.tr: Yunan matematik şaheserlerini korudu — birçoğu yalnızca onun Arapça versiyonlarında günümüze ulaştı

key_contribution.en: Sabian star-worshipper who became the greatest mathematician-translator of the House of Wisdom. His Arabic Archimedes and Apollonius saved irreplaceable Greek mathematics. Extended Pythagorean theorem to general triangles
key_contribution.tr: Beytü'l-Hikme'nin en büyük matematikçi-çevirmeni olan Sâbiî yıldız tapıcısı. Arapça Arşimet ve Apollonios'u kurtararak Yunan matematiğini korudu
key_contribution.ar: صابئي عابد نجوم أصبح أعظم مترجم-رياضي في بيت الحكمة. حفظ رياضيات يونانية لا تُعوَّض

scholarly_context.en: Thabit was a Sabian (star-worshipper) from Harran — living proof of the House of Wisdom's religious pluralism. He translated Archimedes' works that survive only in his Arabic, extended the Pythagorean theorem, and founded a family dynasty of scholars spanning three generations.
scholarly_context.tr: Sâbit, Harran'dan bir Sâbiî'ydi — Beytü'l-Hikme'nin dini çoğulculuğunun canlı kanıtı.
scholarly_context.ar: كان ثابت صابئيًا من حرّان — دليل حي على التعددية الدينية في بيت الحكمة. ترجم أعمال أرخميدس التي لا تبقى إلا بالعربية

historiographical_note.en: Rosenfeld and Grigorian (1993) documented the Thabit dynasty's three-generation scholarly output. Sabra (1968) established the mathematical originality beyond translation.
historiographical_note.tr: Rosenfeld ve Grigorian (1993) Sâbit hanedanının üç kuşaklık ilmi üretimini belgeledi.

modern_parallel.en: A pagan star-worshipper saving the world's mathematics for a Muslim caliph — and then extending it beyond what the Greeks ever achieved.
modern_parallel.tr: Müslüman bir halife için dünyanın matematiğini kurtaran pagan bir yıldız tapıcısı.

transmitted_to.what: Gerard of Cremona translated Thabit's Archimedes into Latin

references.alam: {'name_ar': 'ثابت بن قرة الحراني', 'death_hijri': 288, 'page': 'II/98'}

full_name.en: Abu al-Tayyib Ahmad ibn al-Husayn al-Mutanabbi
full_name.ar: أبو الطيب أحمد بن الحسين المتنبي

significance.en: Most quoted Arabic poet — his verses on heroism, ambition and fate are still memorized across the Arab world
significance.tr: En çok alıntılanan Arap şair — kahramanlık, hırs ve kader üzerine dizeleri Arap dünyasında hâlâ ezberleniyor

key_contribution.en: Most quoted Arabic poet in history — called 'the prophet of poetry' (hence his name, 'one who claims prophecy'). His panegyrics and philosophical verses remain the gold standard of Arabic literary achievement
key_contribution.tr: Tarihin en çok alıntılanan Arap şairi — 'şiirin peygamberi' olarak anılır. Methiyeleri ve felsefi dizeleri Arap edebiyat başarısının altın standardı olmaya devam eder
key_contribution.ar: أكثر شاعر عربي اقتباسًا في التاريخ — لُقّب 'نبي الشعر'. قصائده في المديح والفلسفة لا تزال المعيار الذهبي للأدب العربي

scholarly_context.en: Mutanabbi served at the Hamdanid court of Sayf al-Dawla in Aleppo during its golden age. His qaṣā'id (odes) combine philosophical depth with verbal pyrotechnics. Source material notes he was 'killed at age 50' — assassinated while fleeing Baghdad after a dispute. His poetry has generated more commentary than any other Arabic poet.
scholarly_context.tr: Mütenebbî, altın çağında Halep'teki Hamdani sarayında hizmet etti.
scholarly_context.ar: خدم المتنبي في بلاط سيف الدولة الحمداني في حلب. قصائده تجمع بين العمق الفلسفي والبراعة اللفظية. قُتل وهو في الخمسين

historiographical_note.en: Blachère's 1935 study remains the standard Western critical analysis. Mutanabbi's verse is the most commented-upon corpus in Arabic literature — over 40 known commentaries exist.
historiographical_note.tr: Blachère'in 1935 çalışması standart Batı kritik analizi olmaya devam eder.

modern_parallel.en: Shakespeare if Shakespeare were also a warrior, a political exile, and murdered at 50 while fleeing a city that loved and hated him in equal measure.
modern_parallel.tr: Shakespeare olsaydı — ama aynı zamanda savaşçı, siyasi sürgün ve onu eşit ölçüde seven ve nefret eden bir şehirden kaçarken 50 yaşında öldürülen.

inherited_from.what: Pre-Islamic qasida tradition, Abu Tammam's neo-classicism

transmitted_to.what: Gold standard of Arabic poetic achievement
Modern Palestinian poetry draws on Mutanabbi's resistance themes

references.alam: {'name_ar': 'المتنبي، أحمد بن الحسين', 'death_hijri': 354, 'page': 'I/115'}

full_name.en: Khalid ibn Yazid ibn Muawiya
full_name.ar: خالد بن يزيد بن معاوية

active_places.role: Umayyad prince; first Muslim patron of translation

title.en: Commissioned first Arabic translations of Greek and Coptic alchemical texts
title.tr: Yunanca ve Kıptîce simya metinlerinin ilk Arapça çevirilerini sipariş etti
title.ar: أمر بأول ترجمات عربية لنصوص الخيمياء اليونانية والقبطية

significance.en: First known patron of systematic scientific translation into Arabic — predating the House of Wisdom by over a century
significance.tr: Arapçaya sistematik bilimsel çevirinin bilinen ilk hamisi — Beytü'l-Hikme'den bir yüzyıldan fazla önce

key_contribution.en: First Muslim patron of scientific translation — commissioned Greek and Coptic alchemical texts into Arabic over a century before the House of Wisdom, igniting the translation movement
key_contribution.tr: Bilimsel çevirinin ilk Müslüman hamisi — Beytü'l-Hikme'den bir yüzyıldan fazla önce Yunanca ve Kıptîce simya metinlerini Arapçaya çevirtmeyi sipariş ederek çeviri hareketini ateşledi
key_contribution.ar: أول راعٍ مسلم للترجمة العلمية — أمر بترجمة نصوص الخيمياء قبل بيت الحكمة بأكثر من قرن

scholarly_context.en: Khalid was an Umayyad prince who renounced political ambition for scholarship. Source material identifies him as commissioning the first Arabic alchemical translations from Greek and Coptic. The Jabirian corpus later credited him as a foundational figure. His patronage demonstrates that the translation movement predated the Abbasids.
scholarly_context.tr: Hâlid, siyasi hırsı bilim için terk eden bir Emevi prensiydi.
scholarly_context.ar: كان خالد أميرًا أمويًا تخلى عن الطموح السياسي لصالح العلم. أمر بأول ترجمات عربية للخيمياء من اليونانية والقبطية

historiographical_note.en: The historicity of Khalid's alchemical patronage was debated by Ruska (1924) but largely accepted by Ullmann (1972) and Sezgin (GAS IV).
historiographical_note.tr: Hâlid'in simya hamiliğinin tarihselliği tartışmalıdır ama Ullmann (1972) ve Sezgin (GAS IV) tarafından kabul edilmiştir.

modern_parallel.en: A prince who gave up the throne to fund scientific research — and accidentally started the translation movement that would preserve world civilization.
modern_parallel.tr: Bilimsel araştırmayı finanse etmek için tahttan vazgeçen bir prens — ve dünya medeniyetini koruyacak çeviri hareketini kazara başlatan.

transmitted_to.what: Jabirian corpus credits Khalid as teacher
Translation patronage model adopted by al-Ma'mun

references.alam: {'name_ar': 'خالد بن يزيد بن معاوية', 'death_hijri': 85, 'page': 'II/299'}

full_name.en: Abu al-Qasim Abbas ibn Firnas
full_name.ar: أبو القاسم عباس بن فرناس

active_places.role: Polymath at the court of Abd al-Rahman II

significance.en: First documented controlled flight attempt — glided for about 10 minutes from a hill near Córdoba, 600 years before Leonardo da Vinci's designs
significance.tr: İlk belgelenmiş kontrollü uçuş denemesi — Kurtuba yakınlarında bir tepeden yaklaşık 10 dakika süzüldü, Leonardo'dan 600 yıl önce

key_contribution.en: First human flight attempt — built wings and glided from a hill near Córdoba for approximately 10 minutes, landing with injuries. Also created a planetarium with artificial thunder and lightning, and manufactured clear glass (rock crystal)
key_contribution.tr: İlk insan uçuş denemesi — kanatlar inşa edip Kurtuba yakınlarında bir tepeden yaklaşık 10 dakika süzüldü. Ayrıca yapay gök gürültüsü ve şimşekli bir planetaryum yarattı
key_contribution.ar: أول محاولة طيران بشرية — صنع جناحين وحلّق من تلة قرب قرطبة نحو ١٠ دقائق. كما صنع قبة فلكية بالرعد والبرق

scholarly_context.en: Ibn Firnas was a Berber polymath in Umayyad Córdoba who made the first documented attempt at human flight. He also built a water clock, manufactured clear glass from quartz, and created a room-sized planetarium. The source mentions him in the Andalusia lecture on science as a key figure of early Andalusian innovation.
scholarly_context.tr: İbn Firnâs, ilk belgelenmiş insan uçuşu denemesini yapan Emevi Kurtuba'sında Berberî bir polimâttı.
scholarly_context.ar: كان ابن فرناس عالمًا أمازيغيًا في قرطبة الأموية قام بأول محاولة موثقة للطيران البشري. صنع أيضًا ساعة مائية وزجاجًا شفافًا من الكوارتز

historiographical_note.en: Al-Maqqari's Nafh al-Tib (17th c.) is the primary source. The Baghdad airport Ibn Firnas statue and the lunar crater named after him attest to his enduring fame. Some scholars question whether the flight lasted 10 minutes or was briefer.
historiographical_note.tr: el-Makkarî'nin Nefhu't-Tîb'i (17. yy.) birincil kaynaktır.

modern_parallel.en: The Wright Brothers — but 1,000 years earlier, alone, and using handmade wings from a hilltop in Spain.
modern_parallel.tr: Wright Kardeşler — ama 1.000 yıl önce, tek başına, İspanya'da bir tepeden el yapımı kanatlarla.

references.alam: {'name_ar': 'عباس بن فرناس', 'death_hijri': 274, 'page': 'III/265'}

full_name.en: Abu Muhammad Ali ibn Ahmad ibn Said ibn Hazm
full_name.tr: Ebû Muhammed Alî b. Ahmed b. Saîd İbn Hazm
full_name.ar: أبو محمد علي بن أحمد بن سعيد ابن حزم

title.en: Ṭawq al-Ḥamāma (The Ring of the Dove)

significance.en: Treatise on love — one of the most influential works on courtly love, predating European troubadour tradition
significance.tr: Aşk üzerine risale — saray aşkı üzerine en etkili eserlerden biri, Avrupa trubadur geleneğinden önce

significance.en: First systematic comparative study of world religions
significance.tr: Dünya dinlerinin ilk sistematik karşılaştırmalı çalışması

key_contribution.en: Wrote The Ring of the Dove — a treatise on love that influenced the European courtly love tradition. Also produced the first systematic comparative study of world religions, and championed the Zahiri (literalist) school of Islamic law
key_contribution.tr: Tavku'l-Hamâme'yi yazdı — Avrupa saray aşkı geleneğini etkileyen bir aşk risalesi. Ayrıca dünya dinlerinin ilk sistematik karşılaştırmalı çalışmasını üretti
key_contribution.ar: كتب طوق الحمامة — رسالة في الحب أثّرت في تقليد الحب النبيل الأوروبي. وأنتج أول دراسة منهجية مقارنة للأديان

scholarly_context.en: Source material highlights Ibn Hazm in the Andalusia richLectures as author of The Ring of the Dove (1022). His Fisal is the first attempt at systematic comparative religion — examining Islam, Christianity, Judaism, Zoroastrianism and other faiths with analytical rigor. He also championed the Zahiri (literalist) school of Islamic jurisprudence.
scholarly_context.tr: Kaynak materyali İbn Hazm'ı Endülüs derslerinde Tavku'l-Hamâme'nin yazarı olarak öne çıkarır.
scholarly_context.ar: يبرزه المصدر كمؤلف لطوق الحمامة. والفصل أول محاولة لعلم مقارنة الأديان — يفحص الإسلام والمسيحية واليهودية والزرادشتية بدقة تحليلية

historiographical_note.en: Nykl's 1931 translation of Tawq al-Hamama introduced Ibn Hazm to Western literary criticism. Arnaldez (1956) established the philosophical originality of al-Fisal.
historiographical_note.tr: Nykl'ın 1931 Tavku'l-Hamâme çevirisi İbn Hazm'ı Batı edebiyat eleştirisine tanıttı.

modern_parallel.en: Imagine writing both the definitive book on love (imagine The Art of Love) and the first comparative religion textbook — in the same lifetime, in medieval Spain.
modern_parallel.tr: Hem aşk üzerine kesin kitabı hem de ilk karşılaştırmalı din ders kitabını yazmayı düşünün — aynı ömürde, ortaçağ İspanya'sında.

transmitted_to.what: Courtly love concepts transmitted via Andalusia
De Amore parallels Ring of the Dove

references.alam: {'name_ar': 'ابن حزم، علي بن أحمد', 'death_hijri': 456, 'page': 'IV/254'}

full_name.en: Shihab al-Din Abu al-Fadl Ahmad ibn Ali ibn Hajar al-Asqalani
full_name.tr: Şihâbeddin Ebü'l-Fazl Ahmed b. Ali İbn Hacer el-Askalânî
full_name.ar: شهاب الدين أبو الفضل أحمد بن علي ابن حجر العسقلاني

active_places.role: Chief Shafi'i judge; greatest hadith scholar

title.en: Fatḥ al-Bārī (Conquest of the Creator — commentary on Sahih al-Bukhari)

significance.en: Most authoritative commentary on Sahih al-Bukhari — 13 volumes took 25 years to write
significance.tr: Sahîh-i Buhârî'nin en muteber şerhi — 13 cilt, 25 yılda yazıldı

key_contribution.en: Wrote the definitive commentary on Sahih al-Bukhari (Fath al-Bari) — the most authoritative explanation of the most authoritative hadith collection. 13 volumes, 25 years of work
key_contribution.tr: Sahîh-i Buhârî'nin kesin şerhini (Fethu'l-Bârî) yazdı — en muteber hadis koleksiyonunun en muteber açıklaması. 13 cilt, 25 yıllık çalışma
key_contribution.ar: كتب الشرح الحاسم لصحيح البخاري (فتح الباري) — أوثق شرح لأوثق مجموعة حديث. ١٣ مجلدًا في ٢٥ عامًا

scholarly_context.en: Ibn Hajar was the greatest hadith scholar of the Mamluk era. His Fath al-Bari took 25 years to write and is considered the definitive explanation of Bukhari's collection. Source material mentions him alongside Maqrizi and Suyuti as the intellectual pillars of Mamluk Cairo.
scholarly_context.tr: İbn Hacer, Memlük döneminin en büyük hadis alimiydi.
scholarly_context.ar: كان ابن حجر أعظم محدّث في العصر المملوكي. استغرق فتح الباري ٢٥ عامًا ويُعدّ الشرح الحاسم لصحيح البخاري

historiographical_note.en: Fath al-Bari's completion in 1428 was celebrated in Cairo with a public feast funded by the Mamluk sultan — one of the few occasions a book launch was a state event.
historiographical_note.tr: Fethu'l-Bârî'nin 1428'deki tamamlanması Kahire'de Memlük sultanının finanse ettiği kamusal bir şölenle kutlandı.

modern_parallel.en: Imagine spending 25 years writing the definitive commentary on the world's most important hadith collection — and having the president throw you a book launch party.
modern_parallel.tr: Dünyanın en önemli hadis koleksiyonunun kesin şerhini yazmak için 25 yıl harcamayı düşünün.

transmitted_to.what: Fath al-Bari became the gold standard commentary
Still the primary reference for Bukhari scholarship

references.alam: {'name_ar': 'ابن حجر العسقلاني، أحمد بن علي', 'death_hijri': 852, 'page': 'I/178'}

full_name.en: Mustafa ibn Abdullah Katip Çelebi (Hajji Khalifa)
full_name.tr: Mustafa b. Abdullah Kâtip Çelebi (Hacı Halife)
full_name.ar: مصطفى بن عبد الله كاتب جلبي (حاجي خليفة)

title.ar: كشف الظنون عن أسامي الكتب والفنون

significance.en: Bio-bibliographical encyclopedia of 14,500+ works in Arabic, Persian and Turkish — the most comprehensive Islamic bibliography ever compiled
significance.tr: Arapça, Farsça ve Türkçe 14.500+ eserin biyo-bibliyografik ansiklopedisi — derlenen en kapsamlı İslami bibliyografya

significance.en: First Ottoman world geography incorporating European cartographic advances — Ibrahim Müteferrika printed it in 1732
significance.tr: Avrupa kartografik ilerlemelerini içeren ilk Osmanlı dünya coğrafyası

key_contribution.en: The greatest Ottoman encyclopedist — compiled Kashf al-Zunun (14,500+ works catalogued) and Cihannüma (first Ottoman world geography using European methods). Source material calls him 'the greatest Ottoman encyclopedist'
key_contribution.tr: En büyük Osmanlı ansiklopedisti — Keşfü'z-Zünûn'u (14.500+ eser kataloğu) ve Cihannümâ'yı (Avrupa yöntemlerini kullanan ilk Osmanlı dünya coğrafyası) derledi
key_contribution.ar: أعظم موسوعي عثماني — صنّف كشف الظنون (١٤,٥٠٠+ عمل) وجهان نما (أول جغرافيا عثمانية عالمية بأساليب أوروبية)

scholarly_context.en: Source material explicitly calls Katip Çelebi 'the greatest Ottoman encyclopedist.' His Kashf al-Zunun catalogues 14,500+ works across all Islamic sciences. His Cihannüma was the first Ottoman geography to incorporate European cartographic advances (Mercator, Ortelius). Ibrahim Müteferrika later printed both works.
scholarly_context.tr: Kaynak materyali Kâtip Çelebi'yi 'en büyük Osmanlı ansiklopedisti' olarak adlandırır.
scholarly_context.ar: يصفه المصدر بأنه 'أعظم موسوعي عثماني'. فهرس كشف الظنون ١٤,٥٠٠+ عمل. وكان جهان نما أول جغرافيا عثمانية تدمج التقدم الأوروبي

historiographical_note.en: Flügel's 1835-58 edition of Kashf al-Zunun remains the standard reference. Gökyay (1977) established the intellectual context of Katip Çelebi's rationalist reformism.
historiographical_note.tr: Flügel'in 1835-58 Keşfü'z-Zünûn edisyonu standart referans olmaya devam eder.

modern_parallel.en: Imagine one person building both the Library of Congress catalog AND Google Maps — by hand, in the 17th century.
modern_parallel.tr: Tek bir kişinin hem Kongre Kütüphanesi kataloğunu HEM Google Maps'i — elle, 17. yüzyılda — inşa ettiğini düşünün.

inherited_from.what: Hajji Khalifa tradition, earlier bibliographies (Ibn al-Nadim's Fihrist)
Mercator and Ortelius cartography (adopted for Cihannüma)

transmitted_to.what: Printed Cihannüma on first Ottoman press
Kashf al-Zunun remains the starting point for Islamic bibliographic research

references.alam: {'name_ar': 'كاتب جلبي، مصطفى بن عبد الله', 'death_hijri': 1067, 'page': 'V/203'}

full_name.en: Ibrahim Müteferrika
full_name.ar: إبراهيم متفرقة

active_places.role: Founded first Ottoman Turkish printing press

significance.en: Established first Ottoman-Turkish printing press — 17 works in 23 volumes, ~12,700 copies before closure
significance.tr: İlk Osmanlı-Türkçe matbaasını kurdu — kapanmadan önce 23 ciltte 17 eser, ~12.700 kopya

key_contribution.en: Hungarian convert who established the first Ottoman Turkish printing press in 1729 — producing 17 works in 23 volumes (~12,700 copies) before closure. Source material emphasizes he printed Katip Çelebi's Cihannüma and faced resistance from scribe guilds
key_contribution.tr: 1729'da ilk Osmanlı Türkçe matbaasını kuran Macar mühtedisi — kapanmadan önce 23 ciltte 17 eser (~12.700 kopya). Kâtip Çelebi'nin Cihannümâ'sını bastı ve hattat loncalarının muhalefetiyle karşılaştı
key_contribution.ar: مهتدٍ مجري أسّس أول مطبعة تركية عثمانية عام ١٧٢٩ — أنتج ١٧ عملاً في ٢٣ مجلدًا قبل إغلاقها

scholarly_context.en: Source material states '17 works in 23 volumes, ~12,700 copies' and notes 'scribe guilds' opposition' as a factor in the press's eventual closure. Müteferrika also wrote an important treatise on military reform. His press was the first to print in Ottoman Turkish — though Jewish and Christian presses had operated earlier.
scholarly_context.tr: Kaynak materyali '23 ciltte 17 eser, ~12.700 kopya' ve 'hattat loncalarının muhalefeti' bilgisini verir.
scholarly_context.ar: يذكر المصدر '١٧ عملاً في ٢٣ مجلدًا' ويشير إلى 'معارضة نقابات الخطّاطين' كعامل في إغلاق المطبعة

historiographical_note.en: Berkes (1962) contextualized Müteferrika within Ottoman reform movements. The 275-year gap between Gutenberg (1455) and the Ottoman press (1729) remains one of the most discussed questions in Ottoman cultural history.
historiographical_note.tr: Berkes (1962) Müteferrika'yı Osmanlı reform hareketleri bağlamına oturttu.

modern_parallel.en: A Hungarian immigrant who introduced the internet to the Ottoman Empire — except it was the printing press, and it took 275 years to arrive after its European invention.
modern_parallel.tr: Osmanlı İmparatorluğu'na interneti tanıtan bir Macar göçmen — ama matbaayı, Avrupa'daki icadından 275 yıl sonra.

transmitted_to.what: Accelerated knowledge dissemination despite limited output

references.alam: {'name_ar': 'إبراهيم متفرقة', 'death_hijri': 1158, 'page': 'I/64'}

full_name.en: Sherafeddin Sabuncuoglu
full_name.ar: شرف الدين صابونجي أوغلو

active_places.role: Chief surgeon at Amasya Hospital for 14 years

significance.en: First illustrated surgical atlas in Turkish — 140 miniature illustrations of surgical procedures and instruments
significance.tr: Türkçe ilk resimli cerrahi atlası — cerrahi prosedürlerin ve aletlerin 140 minyatür illüstrasyonu

key_contribution.en: Wrote the first illustrated surgical atlas in Turkish — 140 miniature paintings showing surgical procedures, the most detailed surgical illustrations of the medieval world. Source material mentions his 'illustrated surgical manual'
key_contribution.tr: Türkçe ilk resimli cerrahi atlasını yazdı — cerrahi prosedürleri gösteren 140 minyatür resim, ortaçağ dünyasının en detaylı cerrahi illüstrasyonları
key_contribution.ar: كتب أول أطلس جراحي مصوّر بالتركية — ١٤٠ رسمة مصغّرة تُظهر الإجراءات الجراحية

scholarly_context.en: Sabuncuoğlu built on Zahrawi's Tasrif but added 140 original miniature illustrations — the first illustrated surgical manual in any Turkic language. He documented techniques for cauterization, tumor removal, and eye surgery. His illustrations are the most detailed surgical images produced before the European Renaissance.
scholarly_context.tr: Sabuncuoğlu, Zehrâvî'nin Tasrîf'i üzerine inşa etti ama 140 orijinal minyatür illüstrasyon ekledi.
scholarly_context.ar: بنى صابونجي أوغلو على تصريف الزهراوي لكن أضاف ١٤٠ رسمة مصغّرة أصلية — أول كتاب جراحي مصوّر بلغة تركية

historiographical_note.en: Uzel's 1992 facsimile edition brought international attention. The illustrations are now in the Bibliothèque nationale de France and the Fatih Millet Library.
historiographical_note.tr: Uzel'in 1992 tıpkıbasım edisyonu uluslararası dikkat çekti.

modern_parallel.en: A hospital surgeon who painted 140 step-by-step surgical guides — like a medieval YouTube tutorial channel for surgery, illustrated by the surgeon himself.
modern_parallel.tr: 140 adım adım cerrahi rehber resmeden bir hastane cerrahı — cerrahın kendisi tarafından resimlenen ortaçağ YouTube cerrahi kanalı.

references.alam: {'name_ar': 'صابونجي أوغلو', 'death_hijri': 872, 'page': 'III/171'}

full_name.en: Abu Sa'id al-Hasan ibn Abi al-Hasan al-Basri
full_name.tr: Ebû Saîd el-Hasen b. Ebi'l-Hasen el-Basrî
full_name.ar: أبو سعيد الحسن بن أبي الحسن البصري

significance.en: Earliest systematic treatise on free will (qadar) in Islam
significance.tr: İslam'da irade özgürlüğü (kader) üzerine ilk sistematik risale

key_contribution.en: Pioneer of Islamic theology (kalām) and Basran intellectual circle; founded the tradition of rational theological discourse
key_contribution.tr: İslam kelâmının öncüsü ve Basra fikir halkasının kurucusu
key_contribution.ar: رائد علم الكلام الإسلامي ومؤسس حلقة البصرة الفكرية

scholarly_context.en: Hasan al-Basri's circle was the first organized intellectual forum in Islam, producing both the Mu'tazila and the Ash'ari traditions.
scholarly_context.tr: Hasan el-Basrî'nin halkası İslam'daki ilk organize fikir forumuydu.
scholarly_context.ar: كانت حلقة الحسن البصري أول منتدى فكري منظم في الإسلام

historiographical_note.en: His authorship of certain texts is debated; the Risāla ilā ʿAbd al-Malik is among the most securely attributed.
historiographical_note.tr: Bazı eserlerin yazarlığı tartışmalıdır.

modern_parallel.en: Like Socrates — we know him primarily through his students, who founded entire schools of thought.
modern_parallel.tr: Sokrates gibi — onu esas olarak öğrencileri aracılığıyla tanırız.

full_name.en: Muhammad ibn Muslim ibn Ubayd Allah al-Zuhri

historiographical_note.tr: Hadis tedvinindeki resmî rolü sonraki alimler tarafından ittifakla kabul edilir.

modern_parallel.en: Like the first data architect of a civilization — he designed the metadata schema (isnād) that every hadith would carry.
modern_parallel.tr: Bir medeniyetin ilk veri mimarı gibi.

inherited_from.what: Direct hadith transmission from senior Tabi'in

modern_parallel.en: Like an immigrant scientist whose expertise helped build a new capital city.

inherited_from.what: Fiqh and hadith from Ibn Abbas and Aisha

transmitted_to.what: Ibadi jurisprudence → spread to Oman and North Africa

modern_parallel.en: Like Carl Sagan — a popularizer who made complex astronomical ideas accessible.
modern_parallel.tr: Carl Sagan gibi — karmaşık fikirleri herkes için erişilebilir kılan.

modern_parallel.en: Like a National Geographic journalist who spent decades documenting every region.

full_name.en: Muhammad ibn Muhammad ibn Yahya Abu al-Wafa al-Buzjani

modern_parallel.en: Like the mathematician who perfected calculus notation — his standardization became universal.

modern_parallel.en: Like a WHO field medical guide author whose work was plagiarized but became the standard anyway.

modern_parallel.en: Like computing a billion digits of π — except he did it by hand.
modern_parallel.tr: π'nin milyar basamağını hesaplamak gibi — tek farkla elle yaptı.

full_name.en: Sadr al-Din Muhammad ibn Ibrahim al-Shirazi

significance.en: Synthesized Peripatetic, Illuminationist, and mystical philosophy; introduced substantial motion
significance.tr: Meşşâî, İşrâkî ve tasavvufî felsefeyi sentezledi; cevherde hareket kavramını ortaya koydu

key_contribution.en: Creator of Transcendent Philosophy; proposed substantial motion anticipating process philosophy
key_contribution.tr: Müte'âliye Hikmet'in yaratıcısı; cevherde hareket kavramını önerdi
key_contribution.ar: مبدع الحكمة المتعالية ونظرية الحركة الجوهرية

scholarly_context.en: Most ambitious synthesis in Islamic philosophy — unifying Avicenna, Suhrawardi, and Ibn Arabi.
scholarly_context.tr: İslam felsefesindeki en iddialı sentez.
scholarly_context.ar: أكبر تركيب في الفلسفة الإسلامية

historiographical_note.en: His influence is primarily within the Shi'i intellectual tradition.
historiographical_note.tr: Etkisi ağırlıklı olarak Şiî gelenek içindedir.

modern_parallel.en: Like Whitehead's process philosophy — reality is about becoming, not being.
modern_parallel.tr: Whitehead'in süreç felsefesi gibi.

inherited_from.what: Peripatetic + Illuminationist synthesis from Mir Damad

references.alam: {'name_ar': 'صدر الدين الشيرازي', 'death_hijri': 1050, 'page': 'III/201', 'entry_summary_ar': 'فيلسوف إيراني كبير', 'entry_summary_en': 'Great Iranian philosopher'}

full_name.en: Baha al-Din Muhammad ibn Husayn al-Amili
full_name.ar: بهاء الدين محمد بن حسين العاملي

significance.en: Standard mathematics textbook across the Islamic world for 200 years
significance.tr: 200 yıl İslam dünyasında standart matematik ders kitabı

key_contribution.en: Safavid polymath; excelled in mathematics, engineering, and religious sciences; designed Isfahan's water supply
key_contribution.tr: Safevî polimatı; matematik, mühendislik ve dinî ilimlerde üstün; İsfahan su sistemini tasarladı
key_contribution.ar: عالم موسوعي صفوي

scholarly_context.en: Shaykh al-Islam of Isfahan under Shah Abbas I — highest religious authority who was also a first-rate engineer.
scholarly_context.tr: Şah Abbas I döneminde İsfahan Şeyhülislamı.
scholarly_context.ar: شيخ الإسلام في أصفهان

historiographical_note.en: His dual role exemplifies the Safavid integration of rational and religious sciences.
historiographical_note.tr: İkili rolü Safevî rasyonel ve dinî bilimler entegrasyonunu örnekler.

modern_parallel.en: Like a chief justice who is also a civil engineer — designing infrastructure while heading the judiciary.
modern_parallel.tr: Aynı zamanda mühendis olan bir yargıtay başkanı gibi.

references.alam: {'name_ar': 'بهاء الدين العاملي', 'death_hijri': 1030, 'page': 'VII/68', 'entry_summary_ar': 'عالم جامع صفوي', 'entry_summary_en': 'Encyclopedic Safavid scholar'}

full_name.en: Abu'l-Hasan Yamīn al-Dīn Khusrau
full_name.ar: أبو الحسن يمين الدين خسرو بن سيف الدين

significance.en: Five epic poems rivaling Nizami's Khamsa
significance.tr: Nizâmî'nin Hamse'sine rakip beş mesnevi

significance.en: Pioneering work on Indo-Persian literary theory
significance.tr: Hint-Fars edebiyat teorisinin öncü eseri

key_contribution.en: Pioneer of Indo-Persian literary tradition, inventor of the sitar and tabla
key_contribution.tr: Hint-Fars edebiyat geleneğinin öncüsü, sitar ve tablanın mucidi
key_contribution.ar: رائد التقليد الأدبي الهندي-الفارسي ومخترع السيتار والطبلة

scholarly_context.en: Khusrau bridged Persian and Indian cultures at the Delhi Sultanate court, creating a new literary synthesis that combined Sufi mysticism with Indian musical traditions.
scholarly_context.tr: Hüsrev, Delhi Sultanlığı sarayında Fars ve Hint kültürlerini birleştirerek tasavvuf mistisizmi ile Hint müzik geleneklerini harmanlayan yeni bir edebî sentez yarattı.
scholarly_context.ar: جمع خسرو بين الثقافتين الفارسية والهندية في بلاط سلطنة دلهي

historiographical_note.en: Called 'Parrot of India' (Tuti-yi Hind), his role in creating Urdu/Hindavi literary language remains debated among scholars.
historiographical_note.tr: "Hindistan'ın Papağanı" (Tûtî-yi Hind) lakabıyla anılır; Urduca/Hindavî edebî dilinin oluşumundaki rolü tartışmalıdır.

modern_parallel.en: Like a cultural ambassador creating fusion art — think of artists who blend Eastern and Western traditions.
modern_parallel.tr: Doğu-Batı geleneklerini harmanlayan bir kültür elçisi gibi.

inherited_from.what: Nizami's mesnevi tradition and Sufi thought

transmitted_to.what: Indo-Persian poetic forms and musical innovations
